description: Per-playbook section configuration for the public SAMPLE standard form v1.0.0 (issue #343). Deliberately small: two reviewable sections (sec-1, sec-8) covered by playbook topics, plus a structural preamble and one anchor exempted from the form-coverage check, enough to exercise the anchor-map builder / form-coverage / heading-hash-drift gates end-to-end without pretending to be a real contract. Read at runtime by scripts/build_anchor_map.py via the playbook registry (scripts/playbook_registry.py).

coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-preamble: Preamble identifies the parties and agreement purpose. It carries no reviewable legal clause -- counterparty changes here are de minimis (name/address/date corrections). No topic is needed.
coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-9: Section 9 ('Assignment') is intentionally left uncovered in this small sample playbook -- it is registered so the anchor exists (and so the CI drift-gate fixture, which always references sec-8/sec-9, has a real anchor to describe) but carries no standing playbook position for this deliberately minimal example.
